He realized invisible rays were penetrating the glass and causing the fluorescence. He called them “X-rays” (X standing for “unknown”) because their nature was not yet understood.

results.question: Who discovered the structure of penicillin through X-ray crystallography?

results.question: In what year did Anders Jonas Ångström publish his spectral atlas of the sun?
results.model_response:

This work laid the foundation for modern spectroscopy and was the most accurate solar spectrum map of its time.

**Ojos del Salado** is the second highest mountain in South America.

It stands at **6,893 m (22,615 ft)** on the border between Chile and Argentina. It is the highest active volcano in the world and only surpassed by **Aconcagua** (6,961 m / 22,838 ft) in Argentina, which is the highest peak on the continent.

results.question: What year was the Mongolian People's Revolutionary Party founded?
results.model_response:
**1920**

The Mongolian People's Revolutionary Party (MPRP) was founded in 1920. It originated from the merger of two underground revolutionary groups in Urga (now Ulaanbaatar) and was formally established as the Mongolian People's Party on 25 June 1920 (some sources cite 1 March 1921 for its first congress). results.question: What is the name of the cape at the northernmost point of continental Europe?
results.model_response:
**North Cape (Nordkapp)** is the name of the cape at the northernmost point of continental Europe.

It is located on the island of Magerøya in Finnmark County, northern Norway, at coordinates 71°10′21″N 25°47′00″E. While Knivskjellodden (about 1.7 km farther north) is technically the northernmost point on the island, North Cape is the official and most recognized northernmost point of mainland Europe due to its dramatic cliffs and visitor infrastructure.

results.question: What year was the Organization of American States founded?
results.model_response:
**1948**

The Organization of American States (OAS) was founded on **April 30, 1948**, when its Charter was signed in Bogotá, Colombia, during the Ninth International Conference of American States. It officially came into being in December 1951 after the Charter was ratified by all member states.

results.question: Who designed the Phillips Exeter Academy Library?
results.model_response:
**Louis I. Kahn** designed the Phillips Exeter Academy Library in Exeter, New Hampshire.

Completed in 1972, it is widely regarded as one of Kahn’s masterpieces and one of the most important library buildings of the 20th century.

results.question: Who was the architect of the Yale Center for British Art?
results.model_response:
**Louis I. Kahn** was the architect of the Yale Center for British Art.

The building, located at 1080 Chapel Street in New Haven, Connecticut, was one of Kahn’s final commissions. He designed it in the early 1970s; construction began in 1973, but Kahn died in 1974 before it was completed. The center finally opened in 1977. results.question: Who was the first female Chief Justice of Canada?
results.model_response:
**Beverley McLachlin** was the first female Chief Justice of Canada.

She served as the 17th Chief Justice from 2000 to 2017, becoming the first woman to hold the position. She was appointed by Prime Minister Jean Chrétien on January 7, 2000, and retired in 2017. Prior to that, she had been a Justice of the Supreme Court of Canada since 1989.

results.question: In what year was Technical University of Civil Engineering of Bucharest founded?
results.model_response:
**1941**

The Technical University of Civil Engineering of Bucharest (Universitatea Tehnică de Construcții din București – UTCB) was officially founded in **1941** under the name "Institute of Civil Engineering" (Institutul de Construcții). It evolved from earlier civil engineering education programs that had existed in Romania since the 19th century (notably the National School of Bridges and Roads, founded in 1864).

results.question: In what year was Museums of the Far East founded?
results.model_response:
**1899**

The **Museums of the Far East** (Musea van het Verre Oosten) in Brussels, Belgium, were founded in **1899**. They consist of three sites: the Japanese Tower, the Chinese Pavilion, and the Museum of Japanese Art. The complex was originally established as the “Musée d’Extrême-Orient” under King Leopold II.
